elements.formula-2411-234: \begin{equation}\frac{\ud}{\ud t}R_*(t) =e^{C\q M}\left( \frac{\ud}{\ud t}\frac{1}{q_*(\bm y)}+C\frac{1}{q_*}\frac{\ud}{\ud t} \q M\right) \le C \frac{N^2}{q_*(\bm y)}-C\frac{N^2}{q_*(\bm y)}+C\sum_{\omega'\ne \omega}r^{-1}_{\omega,\omega'} \le \frac{C'}{|\ln \delta|}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-370: By integration, we infer that for $t \ge T_\delta$, \begin{equation}R(t) \le R(T_\delta) + \frac{C}{|\ln \delta|} (t-T_\delta).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-371: Now $R(T_\delta) \lesssim \delta^{-2}$ and $\q M = (\delta^2)$, so that \begin{equation}\frac{1}{q_*(\bm y(t))} \lesssim \delta^{-2} + \frac{1}{|\ln \delta |}(t-T_\delta),\end{equation} and so \begin{equation}q_*(\bm y(t))\gtrsim \frac{1}{\delta^{-2}+|\ln \delta|^{-1}(t-T_\delta)}\end{equation} Step 3: Contradiction Due to \eqref{non-exis:decay , we infer from the above bound that \begin{equation}\label{est:N_div} \int_{T_\delta}^{\infty} \q N(t)^2 \ud t \gtrsim \int_{T_\delta}^{\infty} q_*(\bm y(t)) \ud t = +\infty.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-372: Now recall \eqref{eq:Mbis : in view again of \eqref{non-exis:decay , $q_*(\bm y) \lesssim \q N^4$ and so, for $t \ge T_\delta$ \begin{equation}\frac{\ud \q M}{\ud t} \le - \frac{1}{2} \q N^2.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-373: Integrating in time, we conclude that $\q M(t) \to -\infty$ as $t \to +\infty$.
elements.sent-2411-374: But this contradicts that \begin{equation}|\q M(t)| \lesssim \q N^2 \lesssim \delta^2\end{equation} is bounded (and small).
